active_ingredients: Rosuvastatin calcium 5 mg, 10 mg, 20 mg, 40 mg tablets (e.g., Crestor) - prescription only
indications_and_usage: Indicated as adjunct therapy to diet to reduce elevated total cholesterol, LDL-C, ApoB, non-HDL, and triglycerides and increase HDL-C in adults and pediatric patients (>=8 years) with primary hyperlipidemia or mixed dyslipidemia; to slow the progression of atherosclerosis; and to reduce the risk of cardiovascular events in patients with multiple risk factors. It is a high-potency statin. Prescription only.
contraindications: Pregnancy and breastfeeding (per the 2021 FDA update, statins are generally still discontinued in pregnancy and breastfeeding); acute liver failure or decompensated cirrhosis; known hypersensitivity to rosuvastatin.
warnings_and_cautions: Myopathy and rhabdomyolysis - dose-related; risk higher in patients of Asian descent (start at 5 mg) and with cyclosporine (do not exceed 5 mg/day) or with gemfibrozil (do not exceed 10 mg/day); persistent hematuria/proteinuria; small increase in HbA1c; immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy. Liver enzyme elevations. Avoid concomitant antacids within 2 hours of dosing.
adverse_reactions: Headache, myalgia, abdominal pain, nausea, weakness, urinary tract infection; rarely myopathy/rhabdomyolysis, hepatic dysfunction, proteinuria/hematuria, new-onset diabetes.
drug_interactions: Cyclosporine (limit to 5 mg/day); gemfibrozil (limit to 10 mg/day); HIV protease inhibitors with ritonavir (limit doses per label); warfarin (monitor INR); antacids containing aluminum/magnesium (separate by 2 hours); fenofibrate, niacin (high dose), colchicine (myopathy risk).
dosage_and_administration: Adults: start 10-20 mg once daily; range 5-40 mg/day; the 40 mg dose is reserved for patients who do not reach LDL goals at 20 mg. Adults of Asian descent: start 5 mg once daily. Children 8-17 with HeFH: 5-20 mg/day per age and treatment goal. Take any time of day, with or without food. Continue diet and exercise.
overdosage: No specific antidote; supportive care; monitor for muscle and hepatic effects. Hemodialysis is not useful. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
